calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Nov-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION:

Respondent San Francisco Assessor Recorder's demurrer to petitioner Jose Mejia's "ammended [sic] petition for writ of adminisdtrative [sic] mandamus" is sustained on each of its three grounds.

First, the petition is unverified and thus procedurally defective.

Second, a writ of administrative mandamus addresses a "final administrative order or decision made as a result of a proceeding in which by law" a hearing is held, evidence taken and facts determined. (CCP Sec. 1094.5(a).)
The filing and recording of title documents is not such an order or decision, so the petition fails to state a cognizable claim.

Third, petitioner fails to plead that he had an ownership interest in 48 Meda Avenue, San Francisco, when the Chase loan secured by that property was paid off. The petition thus again fails to state a claim.
Respondent points out that petitioner has a pending federal suit against Chase in the Northern District of California that regards 48 Meda: No. 21-CV-01351-HSG. I thus exercise my inherent power to stay petitioner's writ proceeding in this court pending resolution of the federal suit. (OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion To Set Hearing For Amended Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jose Mejia; (415) 290-9149, Pro Per Petitioner.
No other apperances.

Plaintiff did not provide the court with proper notice to contest this motion. Plaintiff states that he does not contest the Court's tentative ruling but that he is only present to hear the Court's order. Court informs Plaintiff that its the court's order to deny petitioner's motion

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PETITIONER JOSE MEJIA'S Motion To Set Hearing For Amended Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.

Petitioner Jose Mejia's "motion to set hearing for amended writ of adminidstrative [sic] mandamus" is denied. Petitioner failed to comply with Local Rule 8.2(A)(2)(a) under which he is to confer with respondent's counsel before moving to set a hearing date. Moreover, respondent has recently filed a demurrer that must be decided before a hearing on the writ petition can be held.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Petitioner JOSE MEJIA (Tel: 415-290-9149) in pro per;
Atty. JAMES F. HANNAWALT, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3913) specially appearing for City and County of San Francisco.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The City and County of San Francisco's "demurrer to petition for writ of mandate/prohibition/ certification" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. That law requires a declaration showing a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and the City's response to the petition is now due by July 29, 2021.
